perturbation.original_text: Information does not include confidential informa tion that: (i) is or becomes generally known and available to the public other than as a result of any breach by Company of this NDA; (ii) is subsequently learned from a third party who is under no obligation of confidentiality with respect to such Information; (iii) wa s known by Company under no obligation of confidentiality prior to disclosure by the State Bar he reunder; or (iv) was generated independently by Company without reference to the State Bar’s Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Information does not include information that: (i) is or becomes generally known and available to the public; (ii) is subsequently learned from a third party; (iii) wa s known by Company prior to disclosure by the State Bar he reunder; or (iv) was generated independently by Company.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'other than as a result of any breach by Company of this NDA', 'who is under no obligation of confidentiality with respect to such Information', 'under no obligation of confidentiality', and 'without reference to the State Bar’s Information', the exceptions become overly broad. If the Company breaches the NDA and the information becomes public, or if the third party breached their confidentiality and information became available, the information should not be excluded. The later sections on liability and remedies are directly contradicted by this weakened exception.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon submission of a proposal to the RFP, or if the State Bar so requests, whichever occurs first, Company will promptly, and at its own expense: (i ) redeliver to the State Bar all copies of the Information in Company’s possession, whether such Information is in written, electronic, digital or other form or format; (ii) destroy any a nd all analyses, compilations, studies, or other documents in any form or format that were prep ared by or for the use of Company which contain or reflect any Information; and (iii) certify such destruction to the State Bar in writing by an authorized officer of the Company who supervised such destruction, as well as the return of all copies as specified above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the exceptions to the definition of 'Information' by removing key qualifications. Specifically, it removes the stipulation that the exceptions do not apply if the information becomes public due to the Company's breach of the NDA or a third party's breach of confidentiality, or if the information was generated by the company with reference to the State Bar's Information. The contradicted text mandates the Company to redeliver or destroy all copies of the Information. With the new changes, the Company could potentially claim that information it obtained due to their own breach (or third party breach) is now exempt from the requirement to return or destroy it, directly contradicting the obligation outlined in the Contradicted Text, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon submission of a proposal to the RFP, or if the State Bar so requests, whichever occurs first, Company will promptly, and at its own expense: (i ) redeliver to the State Bar all copies of the Information in Company’s possession, whether such Information is in written, electronic, digital or other form or format; (ii) destroy any a nd all analyses, compilations, studies, or other documents in any form or format that were prep ared by or for the use of Company which contain or reflect any Information; and (iii) certify such destruction to the State Bar in writing by an authorized officer of the Company who supervised such destruction, as well as the return of all copies as specified above.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon submission of a proposal to the RFP, or if the State Bar so requests, Company will return the Information to the State Bar.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'whichever occurs first', 'promptly, and at its own expense', the detail on the action of returning or destroying the information are omitted. The later clause regarding breach is now impacted by this since, now it's not specified that the Company needs to do it at their own expense, generating and in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company acknowledges and agrees that the reme dy at law for any breach of this NDA is inadequate and that, in additi on to monetary damages, including but not limited to special, incidental, consequential or puniti ve damages, and any other avai lable relief at law, whether based in contract, tort or otherwise, the State Bar will be entitled to specific performance, injunctive relief or any other eq uitable remedy without the need to (i) prove actual damages and/or (ii) post any bond or other security deposit in connection with such remedy.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that upon submission or request, the Company will promptly return or destroy the information at its own expense. The changed text omits 'whichever occurs first', 'promptly, and at its own expense', and the detail on the action of returning or destroying the information. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text discusses remedies for breaches of the NDA, including monetary damages. The omission of 'at its own expense' in the changed text creates an in-text contradiction since it's unclear if the company needs to bear that expense in the changed text, impacting the 'monetary damages' section and who would be responsible for the cost of remedy.

perturbation.original_text: Company's confidentiality oblig ations hereunder will be conti nuing and will survive expiration or termination of the NDA.
perturbation.changed_text: Company's confidentiality obligations hereunder will survive expiration or termination of the NDA.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'continuing', the duration of the confidentiality obligation becomes unclear. Later, the NDA mentions seeking protective orders, the omission creates an in-text contradiction since there's no more duration for the Confidentiality obligation, but actions need to be taken as if there was one.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, this NDA will no t prohibit Company from disclosing any Information if required by an appropriate legal or regulatory author ity, provided that, prior to any disclosure: (iii) Company provides the State Bar with prompt written notice of the required disclosure so that the State Bar may seek such protective order as it may deem appropriate; and (iv) disclosure of any Information is limited only to the extent legally required and Company uses its best efforts to obtain assurances that confidential treatment will be accorded such disclosed Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the confidentiality obligations were 'continuing' after termination. The changed text removes 'continuing,' making the duration unclear. The contradicted text requires the Company to provide notice to the State Bar so that the State Bar may seek a protective order. Without the 'continuing' obligation, the duration of confidentiality is uncertain, creating a contradiction as the contradicted text implies ongoing confidentiality, which the changed text no longer explicitly guarantees, thus making the protective order clause hard to enforce since the confidentiality obligation may not exist anymore.

perturbation.original_text: This NDA will be governed and constr ued in all respects in accordan ce with the laws of the State of California without regard to its conflict of laws principles.
perturbation.changed_text: This NDA will be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'in all respects' and 'without regard to its conflict of laws principles', the governing law becomes more limited and subject to interpretation based on conflict of laws. It creates uncertainty and therefore contradicts an assumption implicit in the agreement that all aspects of the agreement will be controlled by California law without considering other jurisdiction's laws.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company acknowledges and agrees that the reme dy at law for any breach of this NDA is inadequate and that, in additi on to monetary damages, including but not limited to special, incidental, consequential or puniti ve damages, and any other avai lable relief at law, whether based in contract, tort or otherwise, the State Bar will be entitled to specific performance, injunctive relief or any other eq uitable remedy without the need to (i) prove actual damages and/or (ii) post any bond or other security deposit in connection with such remedy.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the NDA would be governed by California law 'in all respects' and 'without regard to its conflict of laws principles.' The changed text removes this language, making the governing law potentially subject to conflict of laws principles. The contradicted text grants specific performance and injunctive relief, indicating an assumption of consistent and predictable application of California law. By making the governing law subject to conflict of laws, the certainty assumed in the contradicted text regarding remedies under California law is undermined, because it is possible a court could determine that another jurisdiction's law applies and those specific remedies would not be available. The change creates a direct conflict, because the assumption that California law applies is now subject to limitations not present in the original agreement, contradicting the understanding of available remedies described in the contradicted text.
